name: Apple Pear Cake
recipeInstructions:
Or you can use 3/4 cup regular milk and 1 tablespoon vinegar to make sour milk.
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F. Spray a 9‑inch tube pan with nonstick spray.
2. In a large bowl, cream the margarine with the brown sugar until smooth.
3. Beat in the egg and vanilla extract.
4. In a medium bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Stir to blend. Add cinnamon and nutmeg; stir again.
5. Finely chop the apples and mince the dried pears (about 3 ounces).
6. Alternately add the dry ingredients and buttermilk (or sour milk) to the butter mixture in three additions, mixing just until no visible flour remains. Fold in the chopped apples and pears.
7. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 40 minutes, or until golden brown and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
8. Remove from the oven and cool in the pan on a rack for 10 minutes. Then turn the cake onto a wire rack to cool completely.
9. Dust with confectioners sugar just before serving.
Note: If dried pears are unavailable, substitute raisins of similar quantity.
